scope: Viewport surface reconstruction only. The Wave/Bump layer appearance does not alter geometry and is not a slicer or G-code toolpath.

geometry.role: The 28-node graph wraps the supplied high-resolution Benchy mesh; it does not procedurally generate the hull.

capture.lightScaleInterpretation: Blender Area energy and Three.js RectAreaLight intensity use different units. Each backend keeps the same normalized rig positions and sizes; the recorded scales align mean surface exposure rather than claiming equal physical power.

recoveredGraph.color: POINT-domain col attribute written from the Filament Color modifier input
recoveredGraph.frontColor: Blender Mix Color COLOR branch; its two HSV inputs share hue and saturation, so the authored front color remains col
recoveredGraph.backColor: Geometry Backfacing selects the authored secondary Principled branch

interpretation: The same portable filament reconstruction validated on the procedural bolt also renders the dense, supplied Benchy wrapper without asset-specific shader code. Exact topology, near-identical silhouette, aligned exposure, the col branch, Generated-coordinate Wave texture, and Bump parameters are recovered. Eevee and Three.js still differ at subpixel layer bands and grazing light response, so renderer identity is not claimed.
